symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive exertional dyspnoea and severe fatigue, acutely worse, with resting tachypnoea/hypoxaemia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive exertional dyspnoea with functional decline is a core symptom of decompensating heart failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: New orthopnoea (three pillows) with nocturnal cough

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Orthopnoea reflects raised pulmonary venous pressure and pulmonary congestion in decompensation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild bilateral ankle oedema by evening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dependent oedema reflects systemic venous congestion accompanying the failing ventricle.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain and no palpitations

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of chest pain lowers but does not exclude an ischaemic contributor; the picture is congestive rather than ischaemic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Apical pansystolic blowing murmur radiating to the axilla, displaced hyperdynamic apex, soft S1, S3, and bibasal crackles

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An apical pansystolic murmur radiating to the axilla with a displaced hyperdynamic apex and S3 is the classic examination signature of severe mitral regurgitation with volume overload.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe regurgitant jet with LV dilatation/dysfunction on echocardiography

investigation_details.expected_result: Severe mitral regurgitation with a large regurgitant jet, dilated left atrium/ventricle, and assessment of LV systolic function
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: MR severity and LV function are not graded and the need/timing of valve surgery cannot be determined.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Echocardiography confirms and grades the mitral regurgitation and LV impact.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Severe mitral regurgitation with decompensated LV failure
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Progressive exertional dyspnoea, new orthopnoea/nocturnal cough and ankle oedema, with (sealed) classic MR auscultatory signs and resting hypoxaemia, indicate decompensated severe MR requiring urgent same-day assessment, diuresis and echocardiography with surgical referral.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Grades MR severity and LV function and drives the valve-surgery decision; arranged urgently as part of the acute assessment.
Support the heart-failure diagnosis, gauge severity and guide safe diuresis.
Assess rhythm/ischaemia and confirm pulmonary congestion/cardiomegaly.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Remote diuretic prescription without bloods or a scan
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Unmonitored diuresis is unsafe and delays definitive assessment; in-person assessment and echocardiography are required.
